# Let's attempt to read both possible filenames
import os, json, re, sys, textwrap, unicodedata, pathlib

paths = ["/mnt/data/text_to_analyse.txt", "/mnt/data/file-EJMXjFRAP7R8aVG6Uvy1hp-Q052.txt"]
existing = [p for p in paths if os.path.exists(p)]
existing, [ (p, os.path.getsize(p)) for p in existing ]

text_path = "/mnt/data/file-EJMXjFRAP7R8aVG6Uvy1hp-Q052.txt"
with open(text_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text)

# find all quoted substrings using straight double quote character "
quoted_spans = []
for m in re.finditer(r'"([^"]+)"', text):
    span = (m.start(1), m.end(1), m.group(1))
    quoted_spans.append(span)
quoted_spans, len(quoted_spans)

# find text in parentheses preceded by "قال الله تعالى ("
paren_spans = []
for m in re.finditer(r'قال الله تعالى\s*\(([^)]+)\)', text):
    paren_spans.append((m.start(1), m.end(1), m.group(1)))
paren_spans

# find the truncated last citation: after "آدم وزوجة (" capture until newline or EOF
m = re.search(r'آدم وزوجة\s*\((.+)$', text, flags=re.DOTALL)
trunc_span = None
if m:
    # captured includes trailing newlines and references; we need to cut off at a newline before "References:"
    captured = m.group(1)
    # Determine end index relative to full text: start index of group 1
    start_idx = m.start(1)
    # Find end before the line that starts with "References:"
    # Let's find the occurrence of "\nReferences:" after start_idx
    ref_pos = text.find("\nReferences:", start_idx)
    if ref_pos != -1:
        end_idx = ref_pos  # end before newline that starts "References:"
    else:
        end_idx = m.end(1)
    trunc_span = (start_idx, end_idx, text[start_idx:end_idx])
trunc_span

# Let's verify that the indices align properly by checking surrounding context for each citation.

def context_slice(text, start, end, pad=20):
    s = max(0, start - pad)
    e = min(len(text), end + pad)
    return text[s:start] + "[" + text[start:end] + "]" + text[end:e]

spans = quoted_spans + paren_spans + ([trunc_span] if trunc_span else [])
contexts = [context_slice(text, s,e) for (s,e,_) in spans]
contexts

import json

citations = []

# 1) 49:13
s1, e1, t1 = quoted_spans[0]
citations.append({
    "span_start": s1,
    "span_end": e1,
    "span_text": t1,
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "is_valid": True,
    "aya_reference": {
        "sura_name": "الحجرات",
        "sura_number": 49,
        "aya_number": 13
    },
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": t1,
    "completely_invalid": False
})

# 2) Hadith
s2, e2, t2 = quoted_spans[1]
citations.append({
    "span_start": s2,
    "span_end": e2,
    "span_text": t2,
    "citation_type": "Hadeeth",
    "is_valid": True,
    "aya_reference": {},
    "hadeeth_reference": {
        "collection_name": "Sunan Abi Dawud",
        "book_number": 1,
        "hadith_number": 236
    },
    "corrected_text": t2,
    "completely_invalid": False
})

# 3) 2:228
s3, e3, t3 = paren_spans[0]
citations.append({
    "span_start": s3,
    "span_end": e3,
    "span_text": t3,
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "is_valid": True,
    "aya_reference": {
        "sura_name": "البقرة",
        "sura_number": 2,
        "aya_number": 228
    },
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": t3,
    "completely_invalid": False
})

# 4) 2:35 (truncated)
s4, e4, t4 = trunc_span
citations.append({
    "span_start": s4,
    "span_end": e4,
    "span_text": t4,
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "is_valid": True,
    "aya_reference": {
        "sura_name": "البقرة",
        "sura_number": 2,
        "aya_number": 35
    },
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": "وَقُلْنَا يَا آدَمُ اسْكُنْ أَنْتَ وَزَوْجُكَ الْجَنَّةَ وَكُلَا مِنْهَا رَغَدًا حَيْثُ شِئْتُمَا وَلَا تَقْرَبَا هَـٰذِهِ الشَّجَرَةَ فَتَكُونَا مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ",
    "completely_invalid": False
})

print(json.dumps({"citations": citations}, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
